summary: Frontend-only verification of 4 UX fixes on client surfaces (Expo SDK 54, viewport 390x800). ALL 4 fixes confirmed working: (1) DECISION ACTIONS on /client/projects/proj_cfab8370b452 — testID decision-block found, 1 deliverable card 'Push Notifications — review build' with approve-{id}+reject-{id}; computed widths Approve=136px / Reject=138px → diff 2px (≤5px tolerance PASS); both buttons height 40px = single line (no 2-line wrap); Ionicons checkmark on Approve + refresh on Reject rendered. (2) 'Improve your product' add block — testID open-module-catalog renders horizontally (350x77 box) with icon column + text column + Browse CTA on the right; title 'Improve your product' single line at 163x17px, subtitle wraps 2 lines normally — NO letter-by-letter vertical break. (3) Catalog flow — tapping add-module-analytics → POST /api/client/projects/{pid}/modules/add succeeded → testID catalog-success-chip rendered ('✓ Module added to your project · returning…') + Add button text mutated to 'Added' with checkmark icon; auto-navigation back after ~1.1s confirmed (URL → /client/home). (4) client-opportunity-feed on /client/home — testID opp-success-receipt appears as green card after tapping opp-accept-{id} with text 'Added · Two-Factor Authentication / Invoice INV-OPP-C867D5 · $400'; primary CTA 'Add now · $400' fits single line (202px wide, 39px tall, ratio ~2:1 vs 'Not now' 113px). Backend POSTs verified: /accept returned invoice_number INV-OPP-C867D5 + amount $400. ONE MINOR issue: on web preview at 390px width, the 'Request changes' label inside the reject button is text-truncated to 'Request cha…' because react-native-web does not honor adjustsFontSizeToFit; on native iOS/Android the prop will downscale font and render full text — confirm in Expo Go or shorten copy.

ui_bugs.issue: On web preview (390px), text truncates to 'Request cha…' because adjustsFontSizeToFit is a no-op on react-native-web. Both buttons are visually consistent (40px height, 2px width diff) so no 2-line wrap, but the full word 'changes' is cut. On native it should auto-shrink. Verify in Expo Go or shorten copy to e.g. 'Request edits' / 'Changes'.

issues: Console errors during render: 'Invalid style property of borderColor. Value is var(--t-primary)33 / 44 — only single values are supported' — react-native-web cannot concatenate a CSS variable with a hex-alpha suffix. Not introduced by this fix but worth a follow-up sweep of theme-token usage.

action_items: Optional: replace adjustsFontSizeToFit on the Reject label with explicit shorter copy (e.g. 'Changes', 'Edits') or a smaller fontSize at narrow viewports — adjustsFontSizeToFit is not implemented on react-native-web so web users will keep seeing 'Request cha…'. Native (Expo Go / TestFlight) will render correctly.

Optional cleanup unrelated to this slice: theme tokens that emit `var(--t-primary)33` concatenations break borderColor on web (logged in console). Move alpha mixing to a runtime helper or pre-computed token (e.g. T.primary33).
Unrelated separate bug observed during testing: GET /api/client/system-actions?limit=15 returns 500 on /client/home. Surface only — not part of this review request, but worth a follow-up issue.
critical_code_review_comments: /app/frontend/app/client/projects/[id].tsx is 985 lines — exceeds 700-line threshold. Recommend splitting into Hero, ProgressEngine, DecisionEngine, ModulesList, AddBlock, ActivityList sub-components. The DecisionActions row alone would benefit from being its own component since wrapping/sizing logic is the source of repeated UX bugs.
catalog.tsx addModule still uses Alert.alert on error path while success path uses inline chip — consistency would be improved by replacing the error Alert with an inline error chip too.
client-opportunity-feed.tsx accept() handler still uses Alert.alert for the failure path — consider an inline error variant of the receipt for visual consistency.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on functional acceptance criteria; 1 minor web-only cosmetic truncation

seed_data_creation: During testing, accepted opportunity 03c4adb7-fec4-49d5-8c30-42510bee92c8 (Two-Factor Authentication) — created Invoice INV-OPP-C867D5 ($400) + module on project proj_cfab8370b452 for client@atlas.dev. Also added 'analytics' module via catalog on the same project. These can be cleaned up via admin or DB if the seeded fixture matters for next iteration.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Client login client@atlas.dev/client123 → cookie session_token. Project under test proj_cfab8370b452 (title 'Mobile App Refresh'). Opportunity feed renders on /client/home (NOT on /client/projects/[id]). Catalog at /client/modules/catalog?projectId=...&projectTitle=... — back nav after add returns to wherever user came from (router.back). All 3 catalog slugs (2fa, payments, analytics) seeded. testIDs to use: decision-block, decision-{id}, approve-{id}, reject-{id}, open-module-catalog, catalog-card-{slug}, add-module-{slug}, catalog-success-chip, client-opportunity-feed, opp-{id}, opp-accept-{id}, opp-dismiss-{id}, opp-success-receipt, cta-primary (Note: review_request mentioned cta-primary but the actual testID is opp-accept-{id}).
rca_of_issue: The original user complaint that 'Request changes' wraps to 2 lines was caused by btn style having no flexDirection: 'row' + no flex constraints on the inner Text. Fix added flexDirection:'row' + flex:1 + minWidth:0 to btn + numberOfLines:1 + adjustsFontSizeToFit to the Text. On native, adjustsFontSizeToFit ensures the word 'changes' shrinks to fit. On web preview the prop is silently ignored by react-native-web, so the text gets clipped with ellipsis instead of wrapping — visually OK (no double-line) but not full. Same applies to 'Add now · $400' on opportunity card — there it fits at 202px because the cta is wider. The 'Improve your product' add block originally rendered vertically because the parent had no flexDirection or width constraints; fix used flex:1+minWidth:0 on the text column and flexShrink:0 on the icon and Browse CTA, allowing the title to take full row width and the subtitle to wrap normally.
